title: MigrationForged payload
description: Emitted by forge(schema_name='migration', ...) on every successful create — both fresh creates (new NNN minted) and idempotent re-runs (existing slug returned without minting). Records that a SQL migration file landed at the canonical + testutil-mirror paths under the recorded migration_number. Consumers: the dashboard's schema-history view, future trained classifier that flags migration-without-test commits, and the chain-close-completeness audit which joins migration commits with their accompanying acceptance-criteria-status retro section. The idempotent flag lets consumers distinguish fresh authoring from re-runs without inferring from event ordering.

migration_number.description: The NNN sequence number assigned to this migration. Allocated as max(filesystem_max, substrate_max)+1 — the highest number across both this checkout's go/internal/db/migrations/ AND every prior MigrationForged in the shared DB — so parallel worktree agents never mint the same number (chain worktree-multi-agent-orchestration-support T3). Strictly greater than every previously-recorded number; the auto-numbering skips gaps (never gap-fill).

slug.description: The kebab/snake-case migration descriptor (e.g. `add_proj_check_constraints`). Matches the file's stem after the NNN prefix.

file_paths.description: The two repo-relative paths the migration landed at: [canonical, mirror]. Canonical is go/internal/db/migrations/NNN_<slug>.sql; mirror is go/internal/testutil/migrations/NNN_<slug>.sql. The two files are byte-identical post-write (forge verifies via hash compare before commit).

docstring_length.description: Length in bytes of the docstring field at create time; 0 when omitted. Lets downstream consumers count migrations that landed without prose context without re-reading the file.

sql_length.description: Length in bytes of the up_sql body at create time. Always >=1 because the schema requires up_sql to be non-empty.

idempotent.description: True when the call was an idempotent re-run (slug already existed; existing row/path returned without minting). False on fresh creates. Optional — older events may omit; treat absent as false.
